title: lichen_l2.c line 239 lichen_l2_recv comment 'Required:' lacks alternative explanation
description:
lichen_l2.c lines 237-240:

```c
/**
 * @brief L2 receive handler
 *
 * Called by net_recv_data() to let L2 process the packet before
 * passing it up to the IP layer.
 *
 * Required: Zephyr's net_if_recv_data() dereferences ->recv without NULL
 * check (net_if.c:5034), so this callback MUST be provided. Cannot be NULL.
 * Compare dummy L2 which also returns NET_CONTINUE when no processing needed.
 */
static enum net_verdict lichen_l2_recv(...)
{
    ...
    return NET_CONTINUE;
}
```

The comment explains why the function exists (NULL deref prevention) but the body is trivial - it returns NET_CONTINUE immediately. The comment does not explain why no processing is needed.

What goes wrong: A reader wonders if the function is incomplete or if processing was deliberately skipped.

Improvement: Add one sentence: 'No L2 processing is needed here because lichen_l2_input() already decompressed the packet before injecting it via net_recv_data().'
